title: Fiber-Rich Hotel Style Potato Curry for Dosa
description: A flavorful South Indian potato curry with zero wastage, featuring mashed and chunked potatoes for the perfect dosa pairing. Fresh coriander leaves, tender stems, and aromatic tempering create a creamy, fiber-rich curry.

subSteps: Peel 700g potatoes and cut into halves or quarters.
Pressure cook with 1 cup water for 3-4 whistles until soft.
Allow to cool slightly.
VALIDATION: Potatoes soft enough to pierce with a fork easily.
Peel warm potatoes carefully.
Mash about 70% into a coarse mash.
Leave about 30% as small chunks (½ inch pieces).
Keep aside in a bowl.
VALIDATION: Mixed texture - mostly smooth mash with visible potato chunks.
Slice 2 large onions thinly (⅛ inch slices).
Deseed 5 medium green chillies and slice finely.
Grate 2 tbsp fresh ginger (not paste).
Finely chop 1 packed cup coriander leaves.
Finely chop tender coriander stems from the bunch.
Measure out mustard seeds (1 tsp), chana dal (1 tsp), urad dal (1 tsp), cumin seeds (1 tsp).
Finely chop 15-20 curry leaves.
VALIDATION: Onions uniformly thin. Green chillies finely sliced. Ginger finely grated, not chunky.
Heat 3 tbsp oil in a thick-bottomed kadai over medium heat.
Add 1 tsp mustard seeds.
Allow them to splutter and pop (15-20 seconds).
Add 1 tsp chana dal and 1 tsp urad dal.
Saute until light golden (1-2 minutes).
Add 1 tsp cumin seeds and saute for 20-30 seconds.
VALIDATION: Mustard seeds have popped. Dals are light golden, not brown. Cumin is fragrant.

steps.instruction: Add aromatic curry leaves and hing.
subSteps: Add finely chopped curry leaves and 1 pinch hing.
Saute for 20-30 seconds.
VALIDATION: Curry leaves are fragrant. The spice aroma is strong.

steps.instruction: Build the flavor base with onions and chillies.
subSteps: Add 2 thinly sliced onions and 5 finely sliced green chillies.
Saute on medium flame for 6-8 minutes.
Cook until onions become soft, translucent, and lightly sweet.
Do not brown heavily.
VALIDATION: Onions are soft and translucent, not crispy or brown. They should feel silky when stirred.
Add 2 tbsp freshly grated ginger.
Cook for 1 minute.
Add 0.25 tsp turmeric powder.
Mix thoroughly for 30 seconds.
VALIDATION: Ginger is fragrant and evenly distributed. Yellow color from turmeric is uniform.
Add the mashed and chunked potatoes.
Add salt to taste (approximately 1 tsp).
Mix thoroughly so onions and spices are evenly distributed.
VALIDATION: Potatoes are well coated with the spiced onion mixture. No dry patches.

steps.instruction: Simmer with water to create curry consistency.
subSteps: Add 1.5 to 2 cups hot water.
Mix thoroughly.
Bring to a gentle simmer on medium heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Stir occasionally.
The potatoes will absorb flavors and become silky.
VALIDATION: Curry is soft and spoonable, not dry. Potatoes are silky and flavors are well absorbed.
Add finely chopped coriander leaves and tender coriander stems.
Mix thoroughly.
Cook for 1 minute.
VALIDATION: Coriander is evenly distributed and becomes part of the curry.

tips: Allowing the curry to rest helps flavors meld beautifully.
subSteps: Switch off the flame.
Add 1 tbsp ghee (optional but recommended).
Add 1 tsp lemon juice (optional).
Mix gently.
Allow the curry to rest for 5 minutes before serving.
VALIDATION: Curry is aromatic and silky. All ingredients are well incorporated.

seo.metaTitle: Potato Curry for Dosa — Hotel Style Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Authentic fiber-rich potato curry for dosa with fresh coriander and aromatic tempering. Zero-wastage South Indian breakfast side dish.
